...
When you’re moving fast with AI in the enterprise, experimenting with new tools, enabling new platforms, unlocking new capabilities, digital security can’t be an afterthought. It has to be married at the hip to everything you’re doing.
...
Enterprise AI looks different depending on where you sit. Some organizations are leveraging third-party platforms. Others are deep in custom development, building their own toolkits, API layers, and governance frameworks. Some are doing both. There's genuine value across that whole spectrum. IMO, what matters is whether you're building something that lasts.
...
Here's what I've learned working in AI enablement: the technology is rarely the hardest part. The harder work is human. Meeting people where they are. Building real confidence, not just awareness. Creating feedback loops that turn early adopters into internal advocates and advocates into a culture. That's the work that actually determines whether AI takes hold in an organization, and it doesn't happen by accident.
...
Alongside that, data readiness might be the most underappreciated variable in enterprise AI success. The teams that spent years building clean, accessible, well-governed data foundations might not have known they were building an AI runway, maybe they did, but that's exactly what they built. When capability is ready to deploy, they're ready to receive it. That kind of preparation is invisible until it isn't.
...
The same is true of infrastructure and architecture. The goal isn't just to use the best tool available today. It's to build in a way where the next great tool is a plug, not a project. Where a new model, a new interface, or a new capability can be incorporated without starting over. That's the difference between adapting to change and seeking it out.
...
Seeking change actively and intentionally only works when your foundation makes it safe to move fast. Data readiness, sound architecture, and genuine human adoption aren't separate workstreams. They're what make AI actually composable at enterprise scale. I’m fortunate to be able to work with some of the best leadership and AI minds every single day. 

Still learning in real time. #AIEnablement #EnterpriseAI #AIAdoption #DataStrategy #ChangeManagement #DigitalTransformation
...
ChatDVN 3.0, Devon’s internally developed AI platform. Management has disclosed that more than half of Devon’s employees use the system daily, which is a critical detail. Adoption at that scale signals cultural buy-in—not a pilot confined to a digital team or innovation lab. https://lnkd.in/giZUP5_s

results.title: JJ Burnam
highlights:
Data Services Lead at Devon Energy
...
Data Services Team Lead Digital Innovation & Data Analytics at [Devon Energy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/devon-energy)
...
I build data systems that make complex business environments understandable, usable, and scalable. Over 17+ years at Devon Energy (Cimarex, Coterra), I’ve evolved from supporting upstream oil & gas applications to leading enterprise data architecture and integration strategy. My work sits at the intersection of data engineering, semantic modeling, and analytics enablement. I specialize in: • Designing logical data architectures that unify fragmented systems • Delivering high-trust analytical data models for business users and application integrations • Enabling self-service access to data across tools and applications • Translating real-world operational complexity into clear, actionable data products More recently, I’ve focused on positioning enterprise data for AI-driven querying and decision support grounded in governed, well-modeled data. What I Care About • Reducing data “interpretation proliferation” in analytics and integrations • Building semantic consistency across systems • Making enterprise data usable, not just available • Bridging traditional data platforms with AI-native workflows

results.title: Devon Energy AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Devon Energy has positioned itself as a leader in AI-driven oil and gas operations, with CEO Clay Gaspar describing himself as an 'AI evangelist' who has made artificial intelligence central to the company's business transformation[1]. The company launched ChatDVN 3.0 within six months of ChatGPT's release, and more than half of Devon's employees now use the AI system daily across geological interpretation, drilling operations, and production optimization[2]. Devon's AI initiatives have generated measurable results, including 3-5% production uplifts through gas lift optimization, 12% reductions in drilling costs, and 15% improvements in drilling efficiency[3]. The company's CTO Trey Lowe has warned that 'companies that don't deploy AI will get left behind' as Devon demonstrates how AI translates directly to operational efficiency and shareholder value[4].
...

How extensively is Devon Energy using AI across its operations?
...
Devon has embedded AI throughout its operations, with over half of employees using ChatDVN 3.0 daily. AI is deployed in drilling, completions, production optimization, gas lift management, methane detection, and legal workflows.
...
has achieved 3-5% production
...
What is ChatDVN 3.0 and how is it used?
...
ChatDVN 3.0 is Devon's internally developed AI platform launched within six months of ChatGPT's release. It's used across geological interpretation, core analysis, completion design, and operational troubleshooting by over half of Devon's workforce.
...
Devon focuses on practical, measurable applications tied directly to production uplift and cost reduction rather than experimental projects. CEO Clay Gaspar emphasizes that AI must create 'more barrels or more dollars' to be valuable.
...

...
I had the pleasure of working with a team of talented data scientists to fine-tune, train and deploy a BERT question-and-answer algorithm. Our goal was to extract and assign context to text from over 5,000 different tiff images daily. I'm proud to say that our model achieved an overall F1 score of 0.9, which is a huge accomplishment! Additionally, I developed decision trees that helped determine the most likely template for different document layouts for each of our providers. This improved our text extraction algorithm and ensured that we're delivering the best possible results. To further enhance our algorithm, I analyzed data to identify the optimal data set of images to assist with model development and training. This allowed us to reduce the size of our training data set and optimize our text extraction algorithm even further. Show less

results.title: Benjamin Hardy
highlights:
Senior QI Geophysicist at [Devon Energy](https://www.linkedin.com/company/devon-energy)
...
With over 19 years of experience in the oil and gas industry focusing on exploration; I’ve been on seismic acquisition vessels and oil rigs but spent most of my time “on the beach”. I found my niche in the oil and gas industry in quantitative interpretation (QI). I enjoy how QI brings together all the strands of exploration: seismic data, well data, petrophysical interpretations and combines them with rock physics to make predictions. For 15 years I worked for Ikon Science in a QI role that I loved. The fast paced and varied nature of consultancy work, together with my change in location from Perth to Houston, means I’ve had the opportunity to work on datasets from every continent on Earth (with the exception of Antarctica). My focus of the past few years has been on onshore US plays (Eagle Ford, Permian, Bakken and Marcellus Basins). I recently started at Shell and I'm very excited about what the future holds there. In 2021 I’ve augmented my skill set with machine learning and artificial intelligence. I completed the Post Graduate Program in Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning: Business Applications, where I was the top student of my cohort with a final score of 99.39%. I attribute that to the fact that Quantitative Interpretation workflows are similar to those in Data Science. Currently I’m very interested expanding myself by applying data science/machine learning techniques to problems in oil & gas, sustainability and the energy transition.
...

results.title: Siemens Energy AI/Machine Learning Engineer Jobs 2026 | Entry-Level & Graduate Hiring - IT patasala
highlights:
### Siemens Energy AI/Machine Learning Engineer Jobs 2026 | Entry-Level & Graduate Hiring
...
Siemens Energy is hiring AI/Machine Learning Engineers for its Gurgaon office under the Digital Products and Solutions (DPS) organization. This full-time opportunity is ideal for entry-level graduates and candidates with up to three years of experience who are passionate about Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing (NLP), and Generative AI technologies. Candidates with strong Python programming skills and academic or project experience in AI/ML are encouraged to apply.
...
As part of Siemens Energy’s Digital Products and Solutions team, you will work with experienced data scientists, software engineers, and product managers to build intelligent applications that support the global energy industry. This role offers excellent exposure to machine learning pipelines, Generative AI, cloud technologies, backend development, and enterprise AI solutions while contributing to innovative digital products.
...
| Company Name | Siemens Energy |
| --- | --- |
| Role | AI/Machine Learning Engineer |
| Qualification | Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, IT, or Related Field |
| Experience | 0–3 Years |
| Eligible Batch | 2023, 2024, 2025 & 2026 Graduates |
| Salary | ₹8–15 LPA (Estimated Market Range) |
| Job Type | Full Time |
| Work Mode | Hybrid |
...
The AI/Machine Learning Engineer will support the design, development, and deployment of intelligent software solutions that enable advanced analytics and AI-driven capabilities across Siemens Energy’s digital platforms. Working with cross-functional teams, you will contribute to machine learning workflows, backend APIs, Generative AI applications, and cloud-based solutions while building practical experience in enterprise AI engineering.
...
- About Us
- Assist in building and maintaining machine learning and basic Generative AI pipelines, including data preprocessing, model training, evaluation, and inference.
...
- Support the development of simple Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and NLP workflows under guidance.
...
- Work on text processing tasks such as data cleaning, parsing, chunking, and embeddings.
...

...
- Help integrate AI models into enterprise applications and support deployment activities.
...
- Debug, test, and optimize machine learning models and pipelines for performance and reliability.
...
- Stay updated with advancements in Artificial Intelligence, Machine Learning, and Generative AI technologies.
...
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or a related field.
...
- 0–3 years of experience or a strong academic/project background in AI/ML.
- Good programming skills in Python.
...

...

...

...

...
